ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder appeared by zoom for Austin Erik Hills (Beneficiary).
William Ashley appeared by telephone for Austin Edward Hills (Successor Trustee).

Mr. Ashley noted that his Client is not present and stated that he was unable to reach him. The Court asked how much is left in the trust and how long it is going to last. Mr. Ashley stated that the trust is comprised of some stock and a business, that he does not have an appraisal of the business and that there is some cash. Mr. Ashley also stated that the business was purchased during the period of the second account, that it was purchased on an installment basis and a down payment, and that it is paid off. In addition, Mr. Ashley stated that the issue of how long the trust was going to last has always been a concern of his Client and Mr. Feder, spoke of the Beneficiary's spending pattern, provided history, spoke of the Beneficiary's medical issues, spoke of the difficulty in approaching certain issues with the Beneficiary, etc. Furthermore, Mr. Ashley stated that as difficult as the approach has been, Mr. Feder has made this much easier, that in February of 2017 they immediately set up a budget, and that they told the Beneficiary that he needed to reduce his spending and get a job. Mr. Ashley went on to say that the Beneficiary has never held a job, but thought that he could run the business, that the Father is probably going to resign and is going to try to get the other Son appointed as the Trustee, that there were legal issues that arose which required legal defense and spoke of the causes for the expenditures. Mr. Ashley also stated that the Beneficiary has moved to Petaluma, that things are now stabilized, that the business is being operated by very competent people and that the Beneficiary is not involved in the operation of the business. The Court noted the amount of the monthly allowance, and stated that there isn't much to account, that the trust is not under court supervision and that it is not inclined to schedule a next accounting. Mr. Feder stated that Edward has worked very hard at giving his Client every opportunity to succeed and to get him to a place where he would be able to operate independently. The Court commended the parties, and acknowledged that Edward has worked very hard and that Erik has come a long way. The First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Motion To File Under Seal (Ca Rule Of Court 2.551) (Redacted)
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: William Ashley for and with Austin Edward Hills (Successor Trustee). Bruce Feder for Austin Erik Hills (Beneficiary).

Matter called. Mr. Ashley thanked the Court for its indulgence regarding the late call to contest the Court's tentative ruling. Mr. Ashley noted that he resides six hours away, that he was driving, and that he was in a "no cell zone".

Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed the tentative ruling on Austin Edward Hills' Motion to File Under Seal (CA Rule of Court 2.551) as follows: The Motion to File Under Seal is denied. A record may be filed under seal only if the court expressly finds facts that establish that (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC, Rule 2.550(d)). The proposed sealing is not narrowly tailored; it seeks to seal the successor trustee's entire supplemental declaration and redacted attorney timesheets previously submitted with the First Account. The timesheets are not submitted with this Motion, so the Court cannot evaluate the claim to confidentiality beyond noting that there is no blanket privilege for timesheets, which are routinely submitted in support of fee requests as required by local rule so that the Court can determine whether the requested fees are reasonable. The supplemental declaration shares information which the Motion argues should be sealed, as to which the Court cannot find a private interest sufficient to overcome the right of public access and much of which is not directly related to the accounting-including the successor trustee's opinions about the beneficiary and his motivations, descriptions of the beneficiary's medical state, advice from the beneficiary's medical care provider. There is no showing that the Trust's payment of the beneficiary's attorney's fees or other unexpected expenses is an overriding interest overcoming the right of public access or that that there is a substantial probability that any such interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed. The Motion does not establish how reference to the existence of a settlement agreement between the successor trustee and an unnamed third party without discussion of its terms would breach such a settlement's confidentiality clause. In any event, parties cannot override the standards of Rule 2.550 by private agreement. Mr. Ashley is to prepare a form of order and submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Mr. Feder stated that he represents Austin Erik Hills but not in the conservatorship anymore and that he was retained after he was discharged. The Court returned the lodged copies to Mr. Ashley in open court. Mr. Ashley requested a continuance on the first account and report of successor trustee currently on calendar for March 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court continued the March 3, 2020 hearing to April 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All documents are to be filed by March 30th including the replacement for the declaration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bart Flood for IMT Associates (Former Trustee).

The Court stated that there should have been Court approval for payment of Kato & Feder's fees, and raised the issue of attorney fees and disbursements. Mr. Flood stated that Kato & Feder is the Beneficiary's Attorney, that this issue has never been raised before, that all of the Parties have proceeded as if this is an unsupervised Trust and that it was brought before the Court because the Beneficiary wanted to be appointed as Trustee. Mr. Flood also stated this was a lot of work, that they prepared an accounting, that the Beneficiary requested that his Father be the Trustee, that there was a lot of communication between the Parties, a lot of unresponsiveness and they spent a lot of time responding to the Beneficiary. The Petition for Approval of Third and Final Report and Account of Former Trustee is taken under submission.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Continued To Oct-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Austin Erik Hills. Ann O'Hara for IMT Associates with Meredith Taylor in attendance.

Mr. Feder stated that it has been over 2 years since the trust was established, that there has been progress, referred to a declaration by Dr. McKay, that the letter makes it clear that Mr. Hills can manage funds, that it is emphasized that Mr. Hill has the ability to recognize when he needs help, that he wants to manage his trust funds, that he has worked with IMT to get to this point, that the IMT fees have added up to $50,000.00 for the last two years which could be saved, that initially Mr. Hills was working with an allowance of $500 per week, that it was increased to whatever he needed, that he pays his expenses and has done fine, that he cooperated when the property was being sold, that they are not aware of any significant shortcomings, that he has shown everything that could be shown given what he has had to work with, that he is currently looking for work, that because he does not have a job does not mean that he cannot manage his money and that he has identified and retained an advisor at Schwab. Ms. O'Hara reported that in regards to the sale of the property there was a discrepancy in the drafting of the trust in that one part says with court authority and one part says without court authority, that Ms. Taylor worked with Mr. Hills, that they agreed on a realtor, that they had general consent to the process, that they were able to move forward without the cost and time constraints of coming to court, that the current amount of the trust is over 1.2 million, that they have bank statements, that they have no objection to the petition but request that if it is granted the court require court supervision. The accounting petition and the petition for approval of modification of the trust were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Continued To Nov-02-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Continued To Sep-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with (petitioner) Austin Erik Hills. Anne O'Hara for and with (trustee) Meredith Taylor of IMT Associates.

The Court asked if notice had been give to Mr. Austin Edward Hills. Mr. Feder reported that he thought notice had been mailed and that he has been in communication with Mr. Hills who signed a waiver of notice which was filed yesterday. The Court expressed concern regarding the amounts of expenditures. Mr. Feder stated that a promissory note had been paid off, that distribution to Austin Erik Hills for living expenses was $9,000.00 to $10,000.00 per month, that the rate of expenses and disbursements is far less at this time and that they have received an informal accounting from IMT which can be submitted to the court. Ms. O'Hara reported that they had to pay substantial attorney's fees, state and federal taxes and that those expenses will not be repeated. Mr. Feder stated that they also filed a declaration from the doctor in support of the petition, that Austin Erik Hills is doing well and is stable, that his medications are being adjusted as necessary, that his ability to manage himself is better than it was and that there have been some procedural glitches but it is not being mis-administered. The Court requested that an accounting be filed no later than September 14, 2015 and that it is to be complete as possible. Ms. O'Hara stated that they would prefer to file a formal accounting and that they could do so by the deadline. The petition for approval of modification of trust was continued to September 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
